attributes.description: Alerts when unacked messages exceed 500 on a queue. Consumers delivered but never acked.
attributes.name: [RabbitMQ OTel] High unacknowledged message count

investigation_guide.blob:
## RabbitMQ high unacknowledged message count

### What fired
Unacknowledged message count exceeded 500 on a queue.

### Why it matters
Unacked messages are delivered but not yet confirmed by the consumer. Growing unacked counts mean consumers are slow, stuck, or crashing before ack.

### Triage
1. Check consumer processing time.
2. Look for consumer crashes.

### Remediation
- Raise prefetch thoughtfully or reduce it to protect memory.
- Fix consumer code.

### Tuning
- 500 threshold; tune to workload.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-rabbitmqreceiver.otel-default
// Unacknowledged: delivered but not yet acked; buildup indicates consumer issues
| WHERE attributes.state == "unacknowledged"
  AND metrics.rabbitmq.message.current IS NOT NULL
| STATS unack_count = MAX(metrics.rabbitmq.message.current)
  BY resource.attributes.rabbitmq.queue.name,
     resource.attributes.rabbitmq.vhost.name,
     resource.attributes.rabbitmq.node.name
// Alert threshold: adjust 500 to tune for consumer processing expectations
| WHERE unack_count > 500
| SORT unack_count DESC
| LIMIT 20
